title: hieracium valdepilosum subsp. elongatum
intro: Hieracium valdepilosum subsp. elongatum, also known as the elongated hawk's beard, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Hieracium valdepilosum subsp. elongatum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
